priority_mapping.0: P0 / This task should not be done in parallel with another.

assumptions: The MACC coordinator already has a working error-code taxonomy (E100-E900) and auto-retry framework in engine.rs.
The task_selector already supports tool_priority, max_parallel_per_tool, and tool_specializations for routing.
The TUI CoordinatorLive screen, AppState, and pause overlay exist and display active tasks, events, and errors.
The Web API exposes ApiCoordinatorStatus with pause context via /api/v1/status.
TaskRuntime already has slo_warnings (BTreeMap<String, SloWarningRecord>) and upsert_slo_warning() method.
Performer.sh and tool runners emit structured events via IPC (heartbeat, phase_result, failed).
The coordinator pause mechanism (pause file + blocking loop + TUI overlay) is fully operational.
All tasks aim to be small enough to complete in < 1 day by a single AI agent.

tasks.title: Define rate-limit error codes, types, and config fields
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: MACC has no dedicated error category for rate-limit / quota exhaustion. The existing error taxonomy (E100-E900) does not distinguish transient throttling from permanent failures, and the coordinator has no types to represent tool-level rate-limit state.
Key actions:
  1. Add E600 error code series: E601 (rate-limited / 429), E602 (quota exhausted / hard limit).
  2. Define RateLimitInfo struct (tool_id, error_code, retry_after_seconds, detected_at, source_header).
  3. Define ToolThrottleState struct (tool_id, throttled_until, consecutive_429_count, backoff_seconds, last_rate_limit_info).
  4. Add coordinator config fields: rate_limit_backoff_base_seconds, rate_limit_backoff_max_seconds, rate_limit_fallback_enabled, rate_limit_throttle_parallel.
  5. Add corresponding fields to CoordinatorEnvConfig for CLI/env override.
Out of scope: Actual detection logic, backoff engine, routing changes, TUI display.
Success criteria: cargo check -p macc-core passes. All new types are Serialize+Deserialize+Clone+Debug+Default. Config fields are documented with serde defaults. Unit tests validate serialization roundtrip for all new types.
tasks.objective: Establish the contracts and vocabulary for rate-limit handling before any implementation begins.
tasks.result: New file core/src/coordinator/rate_limit.rs with RateLimitInfo, ToolThrottleState, and helper functions. E601/E602 documented alongside existing codes. CoordinatorConfig and CoordinatorEnvConfig extended with 4 new fields each. Unit tests pass.
steps: Create core/src/coordinator/rate_limit.rs with RateLimitInfo and ToolThrottleState structs.
Add E600 series constants (E601, E602) to the module with doc comments explaining the distinction (transient vs hard quota).
Add pub mod rate_limit to core/src/coordinator.rs.

Add rate_limit_backoff_base_seconds (default 30), rate_limit_backoff_max_seconds (default 300), rate_limit_fallback_enabled (default true), rate_limit_throttle_parallel (default true) to CoordinatorConfig in core/src/config/mod.rs with #[serde(default)].
Mirror the same 4 fields as Option<T> in CoordinatorEnvConfig in core/src/coordinator/types.rs.
Add resolve helper functions: resolve_rate_limit_backoff_base(), resolve_rate_limit_backoff_max(), etc. following the existing resolve_* pattern in control_plane.rs.
Write unit tests: serialization roundtrip for RateLimitInfo and ToolThrottleState, config default values.
Verify cargo check -p macc-core and cargo test -p macc-core pass.
tasks.notes: This is the contracts-first task. All subsequent tasks depend on these types. Follow the existing resolve pattern: env_cfg -> coordinator_cfg -> default.

tasks.title: Detect rate-limit errors in performer output and emit E601/E602
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: Currently, rate-limit errors (HTTP 429, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, quota exhaustion messages) from AI tools are treated as generic task failures (E101). The performer and coordinator cannot distinguish throttling from real errors.
Key actions:
  1. In core/src/coordinator/engine.rs, extend the job-completion error classification to detect rate-limit patterns.
  2. Parse performer stderr/event payload for known rate-limit signatures: '429', 'RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED', 'rate limit', 'quota exceeded', 'Too Many Requests', 'token limit', 'usage limit reached'.
  3. Parse Retry-After and x-ratelimit-reset headers if present in the error output.
  4. Classify as E601 (transient rate-limit) or E602 (hard quota exhaustion, e.g. monthly cap reached).
  5. Populate RateLimitInfo with retry_after_seconds when header is available.
  6. Emit a rate_limit_detected event via the event bus.
Out of scope: Backoff delay logic, tool routing changes, TUI display.
Success criteria: When a performer exits with output containing '429' or 'RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED', the task gets error_code E601 (not E101). When output contains 'quota exceeded' or 'usage limit reached', error_code is E602. RateLimitInfo is populated. Event is emitted. Unit tests cover all detection patterns.
tasks.objective: Correctly categorize rate-limit failures so the coordinator can apply specialized handling instead of generic retry.
tasks.result: core/src/coordinator/rate_limit.rs gains detect_rate_limit_from_output() and parse_retry_after() functions. engine.rs calls detection before generic error classification. New event type rate_limit_detected added to COORDINATOR_EVENT_TYPES_V1. Tests cover 8+ detection patterns.
steps: Add detect_rate_limit_from_output(stderr: &str, stdout: &str) -> Option<RateLimitInfo> to core/src/coordinator/rate_limit.rs using regex patterns for all known signatures.
Add parse_retry_after(output: &str) -> Option<u64> to extract seconds from Retry-After or x-ratelimit-reset headers in raw output.
Add rate_limit_detected to COORDINATOR_EVENT_TYPES_V1 in core/src/coordinator.rs.
In engine.rs apply_job_completion_in_registry(), call detect_rate_limit_from_output() BEFORE the generic error-code assignment. If matched, set last_error_code to E601 or E602.
Store RateLimitInfo in the task's runtime extra field (serde_json::Value) for downstream consumers.
Emit a rate_limit_detected event with tool_id and retry_after in the event payload.

Write unit tests: detect 429, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, 'rate limit exceeded', 'quota exceeded', 'usage limit reached', 'Too Many Requests'. Test Retry-After parsing (integer seconds and HTTP-date). Test E601 vs E602 distinction.
Verify cargo test -p macc-core passes.
tasks.notes: Detection must be case-insensitive. The regex patterns should be compiled once (lazy_static or OnceLock). E602 is for hard quotas (monthly/weekly caps), E601 for transient 429s.

tasks.title: Implement exponential backoff engine for rate-limited tasks
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: When a task fails with E601/E602, immediately retrying will worsen the rate-limit situation. The coordinator needs a backoff engine that computes an appropriate delay before re-queuing.
Key actions:
  1. Implement compute_backoff_delay() that returns the delay in seconds using: min(base * 2^attempt, max) with jitter.
  2. If RateLimitInfo contains retry_after_seconds (from Retry-After header), use that value instead of computed backoff.
  3. Store ToolThrottleState per tool in the coordinator runtime state.
  4. When a task fails with E601, update the tool's ToolThrottleState (increment consecutive count, set throttled_until).
  5. When a task fails with E602, set throttled_until to a longer period (or indefinite until manual resume).
  6. The existing auto-retry loop in engine.rs should respect throttled_until: if the tool is throttled, do not requeue immediately — set a delayed_until timestamp on the task.
  7. Add delayed_until field to TaskRuntime.
Out of scope: Dynamic routing (fallback to other tools), max_parallel adjustment, TUI.
Success criteria: After an E601 failure, the task is requeued with a computed delay. Consecutive 429s produce exponentially increasing delays (30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, capped at max). Retry-After header value overrides computed delay. E602 pauses the tool indefinitely. Tests validate delay computation and state tracking.
tasks.objective: Prevent retry storms by introducing intelligent delay between rate-limited retries.
tasks.result: core/src/coordinator/rate_limit.rs gains compute_backoff_delay(), update_throttle_state(). TaskRuntime gains delayed_until field. engine.rs respects delays. 6+ unit tests.
steps: Add compute_backoff_delay(attempt: usize, base_seconds: u64, max_seconds: u64, retry_after: Option<u64>) -> u64 to rate_limit.rs. Use min(base * 2^attempt, max) with 10% random jitter. If retry_after is Some, return max(retry_after, computed).

Add update_throttle_state(state: &mut ToolThrottleState, info: &RateLimitInfo, backoff: u64, now: &str) to rate_limit.rs. Increment consecutive_429_count, set throttled_until = now + backoff, store last info.
Add clear_throttle_state(state: &mut ToolThrottleState) to reset when a tool succeeds (consecutive count = 0).
Add delayed_until: Option<String> (ISO 8601) to TaskRuntime in core/src/coordinator/model.rs.
In engine.rs apply_job_completion_in_registry(), when error code is E601: compute backoff, set task.task_runtime.delayed_until, requeue to todo with reason 'rate_limit_backoff'.
In engine.rs, when error code is E602: do NOT auto-retry. Instead, transition to Blocked state with reason 'quota_exhausted'. Emit a quota_exhausted event.
Add quota_exhausted to COORDINATOR_EVENT_TYPES_V1.
Write unit tests: backoff computation (1st=30s, 2nd=60s, 3rd=120s, 4th=240s, 5th=300s cap), Retry-After override, throttle state update/clear, E602 blocks task.
Verify cargo test -p macc-core passes.
tasks.notes: Jitter should use rand or a simple deterministic hash to avoid all tasks retrying at the exact same time. If rand is not available, use a hash of task_id + attempt as pseudo-jitter.

tasks.title: Respect delayed_until during task dispatch selection
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: After RL-BACKOFF-003 sets delayed_until on rate-limited tasks, the task selector still sees them as 'todo' and tries to dispatch them immediately.
Key actions:
  1. In task_selector.rs select_next_ready_task_typed(), filter out tasks where delayed_until > now.
  2. Add is_task_delayed(task, now) helper to rate_limit.rs.
Out of scope: Tool-level throttle state checks (covered in RL-ROUTE-005).
Success criteria: A task with delayed_until in the future is not selected for dispatch. Once delayed_until passes, the task becomes eligible again. Unit tests validate both scenarios.
tasks.objective: Ensure the dispatch loop respects backoff delays and does not re-dispatch throttled tasks prematurely.
tasks.result: task_selector.rs calls is_task_delayed() during candidate filtering. 3+ unit tests.
steps: Add is_task_delayed(task: &Task, now: &str) -> bool to core/src/coordinator/rate_limit.rs. Compares delayed_until against now using ISO 8601 string comparison.
In task_selector.rs select_next_ready_task_typed(), add a filter after the state == 'todo' check: skip tasks where is_task_delayed(task, now) returns true.
Pass current timestamp (now parameter) through the selection function. Extend TaskSelectorConfig or add a now field.
Write unit tests: task with future delayed_until is skipped, task with past delayed_until is eligible, task without delayed_until is eligible.
Verify cargo test -p macc-core passes.
tasks.notes: ISO 8601 string comparison works for chronological ordering (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). No chrono dependency needed for the comparison itself.

tasks.title: Implement dynamic tool fallback routing on rate-limit
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: When the primary tool (e.g. Codex) is rate-limited, the coordinator blocks rather than trying the next tool in tool_priority. The existing pick_tool() in task_selector.rs checks per-tool capacity but not rate-limit state.
Key actions:
  1. Maintain a ToolThrottleRegistry (BTreeMap<String, ToolThrottleState>) in the coordinator runtime.
  2. Extend pick_tool() to skip tools that are currently throttled (throttled_until > now).
  3. When the primary tool is throttled and rate_limit_fallback_enabled is true, select the next available tool from tool_priority.
  4. When ALL tools are throttled, return None (no dispatch possible until earliest throttle expires).
  5. On successful task completion, clear the throttle state for that tool.
  6. Emit a tool_fallback event when a task is assigned to a non-primary tool due to rate-limiting.
Out of scope: Concurrency throttle (RL-THROTTLE-006), TUI display.
Success criteria: When tool A is throttled, tasks are dispatched to tool B (next in priority). When all tools are throttled, dispatch pauses. When tool A recovers, it resumes as primary. Unit tests cover fallback chain, all-throttled, and recovery scenarios.
tasks.objective: Keep the project moving forward by routing tasks to non-throttled tools instead of blocking on a single provider's quota.
tasks.result: ToolThrottleRegistry type added to rate_limit.rs. pick_tool() checks throttle state. tool_fallback event emitted. 5+ unit tests.
steps: Add ToolThrottleRegistry type alias (BTreeMap<String, ToolThrottleState>) to rate_limit.rs.
Add is_tool_throttled(registry: &ToolThrottleRegistry, tool_id: &str, now: &str) -> bool helper.
Add next_throttle_expiry(registry: &ToolThrottleRegistry) -> Option<String> helper (returns earliest throttled_until across all tools).
Extend TaskSelectorConfig with throttle_registry: Option<ToolThrottleRegistry> field.
In task_selector.rs pick_tool(), after building the candidate list, filter out tools where is_tool_throttled() returns true (only when rate_limit_fallback_enabled is true in config).
In control_plane.rs dispatch_ready_tasks_native(), pass the current ToolThrottleRegistry into TaskSelectorConfig.
On successful job completion in engine.rs, call clear_throttle_state() for the task's tool.
Add tool_fallback to COORDINATOR_EVENT_TYPES_V1. Emit when a task is assigned to a non-primary tool.
Write unit tests: primary throttled -> fallback to secondary, all throttled -> None returned, throttle cleared on success, fallback disabled -> no routing change.
Verify cargo test -p macc-core passes.
tasks.notes: The ToolThrottleRegistry should be stored in CoordinatorRunState (runtime.rs) for the async control plane, and passed as a parameter for the sync task_selector. Do not store it in the persistent registry (it's volatile runtime state).

tasks.title: Dynamically reduce max_parallel on rate-limit detection
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: A high max_parallel value saturates TPM (tokens-per-minute) limits quickly. When the first rate-limit is detected, the coordinator should proactively reduce concurrency to smooth out resource consumption.
Key actions:
  1. Track effective_max_parallel (mutable at runtime) in CoordinatorRunState, initialized from config max_parallel.
  2. When a rate-limit E601 is detected and rate_limit_throttle_parallel is true: reduce effective_max_parallel by 1 (min 1).
  3. When a tool recovers (successful completion after throttle): increase effective_max_parallel by 1 (up to original max_parallel).
  4. Log concurrency changes via the coordinator logger.
  5. Emit concurrency_adjusted event.
Out of scope: TUI display of dynamic concurrency (covered in RL-TUI-007).
Success criteria: First E601 reduces parallel from N to N-1. Recovery restores parallel from N-1 to N. Never drops below 1. Never exceeds original config value. Unit tests validate the ramp-down and ramp-up logic.
tasks.objective: Prevent rate-limit cascades by reducing concurrency pressure when throttling is detected.
tasks.result: CoordinatorRunState gains effective_max_parallel and original_max_parallel fields. Dispatch loop uses effective_max_parallel. concurrency_adjusted event added. 4+ unit tests.
steps: Add effective_max_parallel: usize and original_max_parallel: usize to CoordinatorRunState in runtime.rs. Initialize both from config max_parallel.
Add reduce_parallel(state: &mut CoordinatorRunState) -> usize and restore_parallel(state: &mut CoordinatorRunState) -> usize helpers in rate_limit.rs.
In control_plane.rs dispatch_ready_tasks_native(), use state.effective_max_parallel instead of the config value for the dispatch limit check.
In engine.rs, when E601 is detected and rate_limit_throttle_parallel config is true, call reduce_parallel().
In engine.rs, when a job completes successfully AND the tool was previously throttled, call restore_parallel().
Add concurrency_adjusted to COORDINATOR_EVENT_TYPES_V1. Emit with old_value and new_value in payload.
Log via coordinator logger: 'Rate-limit detected: reducing max_parallel from N to N-1' and 'Tool recovered: restoring max_parallel from N-1 to N'.
Write unit tests: reduce from 4 to 3 on first 429, reduce from 3 to 2 on second 429, never below 1, restore from 2 to 3 on success, never above original.
Verify cargo test -p macc-core passes.
tasks.notes: This is deliberately conservative — reduce by 1, not halve. The combination with backoff delays means the system self-heals gradually rather than oscillating.

tasks.title: Display rate-limit status and quota warnings in TUI CoordinatorLive
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: When tools are rate-limited, the TUI shows 'Failed' status which is misleading. Users must dig into logs to understand the situation. The CoordinatorLive screen should show rate-limit state, throttled tools, and dynamic concurrency.
Key actions:
  1. Extend CoordinatorTaskSnapshot with throttled_tools (list of tool IDs + throttled_until).
  2. Extend AppState with coordinator_throttled_tools and coordinator_effective_max_parallel.
  3. In CoordinatorLive rendering, display a 'Throttled Tools' section below the active tasks.
  4. When a tool is throttled, show: 'tool_id: throttled until HH:MM:SS (backoff: Ns, consecutive: N)'.
  5. Show effective_max_parallel vs original: 'Concurrency: 2/4 (throttled)'.
  6. When E602 (quota exhausted) triggers a pause, display a specific banner: 'PAUSED: Quota exhausted for <tool_id>. Resume after quota resets or switch tool.' instead of generic pause message.
  7. In active tasks display, show 'rate-limited' runtime status instead of 'failed' for E601 tasks.
Out of scope: Web API changes (RL-WEB-008).
Success criteria: TUI CoordinatorLive shows throttled tools section. Rate-limited tasks show 'rate-limited' not 'failed'. Quota exhaustion pause banner is specific. Concurrency display shows effective/original.
tasks.objective: Give operators immediate visibility into rate-limit state without needing to read logs.
tasks.result: tui/src/state.rs AppState extended. tui/src/lib.rs CoordinatorLive rendering updated. Pause overlay shows quota-specific message for E602.
steps: Add ThrottledToolInfo struct (tool_id, throttled_until, consecutive_count, backoff_seconds) to tui/src/state.rs.
Add coordinator_throttled_tools: Vec<ThrottledToolInfo> and coordinator_effective_max_parallel: Option<(usize, usize)> (effective, original) to AppState.
In the CoordinatorLive refresh logic, populate throttled_tools from the coordinator status or events.
In tui/src/lib.rs CoordinatorLive render, add a 'Throttled Tools' block between the task snapshot and active tasks. Show each throttled tool with countdown.
Modify the active tasks display: when task.last_error_code starts with 'E601', show runtime_status as 'rate-limited' (amber color) instead of 'failed' (red).
Modify the concurrency display line to show 'Concurrency: N/M (throttled)' when effective < original.

In render_coordinator_pause_overlay(), check if pause_reason contains 'quota_exhausted' or error code E602. If so, show specific message: 'Quota exhausted for <tool>. Options: wait for quota reset, switch to another tool, or resume manually.'
Verify cargo check -p macc-tui passes.
tasks.notes: Colors: rate-limited = amber/yellow, quota-exhausted = red with bold. Use the existing theme system (t.warn for amber, t.bad for red).

tasks.title: Expose rate-limit status in Web API
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Web API (/api/v1/status) does not expose rate-limit state. External dashboards and monitoring tools cannot observe throttling.
Key actions:
  1. Extend ApiCoordinatorStatus with throttled_tools array and effective_max_parallel.
  2. Extend CoordinatorStatus in core with the same fields.
  3. Populate the fields from the runtime throttle registry when building status.
Out of scope: TUI changes (RL-TUI-007), detection logic.
Success criteria: GET /api/v1/status returns throttled_tools array (may be empty) and effective_max_parallel. When a tool is throttled, the API response includes it. Existing tests still pass.
tasks.objective: Enable external observability of rate-limit state via the REST API.
tasks.result: core/src/service/coordinator_workflow.rs CoordinatorStatus extended. cli/src/commands/web.rs ApiCoordinatorStatus extended. Tests updated.
steps: Add throttled_tools: Vec<ThrottledToolStatus> and effective_max_parallel: Option<usize> to CoordinatorStatus in coordinator_workflow.rs. ThrottledToolStatus has tool_id: String, throttled_until: String, consecutive_count: usize.
Add matching fields to ApiCoordinatorStatus in web.rs with #[serde(skip_serializing_if = ...)] for empty/None.
In get_coordinator_status(), populate throttled_tools from the ToolThrottleRegistry if available (read from runtime state or a persisted snapshot).
Update the test fixture in web.rs to include throttled_tools: vec![] and effective_max_parallel: None.
Verify cargo check -p macc-cli --tests passes.
tasks.notes: The Web API is JSON-only. Use skip_serializing_if to keep responses clean when no throttling is active.

tasks.title: Update performer.sh and tool runners to emit structured rate-limit errors
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: Currently, performer.sh and tool runners (ralph_gemini.sh etc.) use ad-hoc grep patterns to detect rate limits (grep -qE 'RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED|429'). This detection is not standardized and does not emit structured error codes that the Rust coordinator can consume.
Key actions:
  1. In performer.sh, after tool execution, run a rate-limit detection pass on stderr/stdout.
  2. If a rate-limit pattern is detected, emit a structured event with error_code E601 or E602 via the IPC channel.
  3. Include Retry-After value if found in the output.
  4. Standardize detection patterns across all tool runners.
Out of scope: Rust-side detection (RL-DETECT-002 handles that independently). This task ensures shell-side also emits clean signals.
Success criteria: When a tool runner encounters a 429, performer.sh emits a JSON event with error_code=E601 via IPC. When 'quota exceeded' is detected, error_code=E602. The existing IPC protocol is used without changes.
tasks.objective: Ensure the shell-based performer pipeline emits clean, structured rate-limit signals that complement the Rust-side detection.
tasks.result: performer.sh gains detect_rate_limit() function. Event emission uses existing emit_event() with error_code field. Works for all tool runners.
steps: Add detect_rate_limit() function to performer.sh that checks combined stderr+stdout for known patterns: 429, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, 'rate limit', 'quota exceeded', 'Too Many Requests', 'usage limit reached'.
Parse Retry-After header value if present in the output.
Classify as E601 (transient) or E602 (hard quota) using the same heuristics as the Rust side.
After tool execution failure, call detect_rate_limit(). If matched, emit a 'failed' event with error_code in the payload JSON, plus retry_after_seconds if available.
Ensure the exit code from performer.sh is non-zero (so the Rust coordinator also processes the failure), but the error_code in the event allows smart handling.
Remove ad-hoc rate-limit grep patterns from individual runner scripts (ralph_gemini.sh etc.) and consolidate into the shared detect_rate_limit() function.
Test manually with simulated rate-limit output.
tasks.notes: The Rust-side detection (RL-DETECT-002) is a safety net — it will also parse the raw output. Both sides detecting the same thing is intentional redundancy, not duplication.

tasks.title: Add E601/E602 to default retry list and document all settings
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: E601 must be in the default error_code_retry_list so rate-limited tasks are auto-retried (with backoff). E602 must NOT be in the retry list (hard quota should block, not retry). All new settings need documentation.
Key actions:
  1. Update the default error_code_retry_list from 'E101,E102,E103,E301,E302,E303' to 'E101,E102,E103,E301,E302,E303,E601'.
  2. Document all new coordinator settings in MACC.md and README.md.
  3. Update the TUI Automation Settings screen to include the 4 new rate-limit fields.
Out of scope: Implementation of backoff or routing (already covered).
Success criteria: E601 is auto-retried by default. E602 is NOT auto-retried. MACC.md documents E600 series, all new config fields, and the backoff/fallback behavior. TUI shows 4 new fields in Automation Settings.
tasks.objective: Ensure rate-limit handling works out of the box with safe defaults and is fully documented.
tasks.result: Default retry list updated in control_plane.rs. MACC.md §3.4.5.1 extended with E600 series. MACC.md §12.3 extended with rate-limit settings. README.md coordinator section updated. TUI Automation screen renders 4 new fields.
steps: In core/src/coordinator/control_plane.rs, update the default error_code_retry_list string to include E601.
In MACC.md §3.4.5.1 (Error codes), add E600 series: E601 (rate-limited / transient 429), E602 (quota exhausted / hard limit).
In MACC.md §12.3 (Coordinator command), document the 4 new config fields: rate_limit_backoff_base_seconds, rate_limit_backoff_max_seconds, rate_limit_fallback_enabled, rate_limit_throttle_parallel.
Add a new subsection §12.3.5 'Rate-limit handling' in MACC.md describing the full flow: detection -> classification -> backoff -> fallback -> throttle -> visibility.
Update README.md coordinator section with brief rate-limit handling description.
In tui/src/lib.rs Automation Settings screen, add input fields for the 4 new coordinator settings following the existing pattern.
Verify cargo check -p macc-tui passes.
tasks.notes: The TUI Automation screen already renders 27 coordinator parameters. Adding 4 more follows the same pattern (scroll list with label + input).

tasks.title: Integration test: full rate-limit lifecycle (detect -> backoff -> fallback -> recover)
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: Individual unit tests validate each component, but the full lifecycle (detection -> backoff delay -> tool fallback -> concurrency reduction -> recovery) needs end-to-end validation.
Key actions:
  1. Write an integration test in core/tests/ that simulates a rate-limit scenario.
  2. Use a mock task registry with 3 tasks and 2 tools.
  3. Simulate E601 on tool A, verify: task requeued with delay, tool A throttled, next dispatch uses tool B, effective_max_parallel reduced.
  4. Simulate E602 on tool B, verify: task blocked (not retried), pause event emitted.
  5. Simulate successful completion on tool A, verify: throttle cleared, max_parallel restored.
Out of scope: TUI/Web display testing (visual, not automatable here).
Success criteria: Integration test passes end-to-end. All state transitions are verified. Test runs in < 10 seconds.
tasks.objective: Validate the complete rate-limit handling pipeline works correctly when all components are wired together.
tasks.result: New file core/tests/rate_limit_integration.rs with 3 test scenarios covering the full lifecycle.
steps: Create core/tests/rate_limit_integration.rs.
Build a mock TaskRegistry with 3 tasks (todo state) and a TaskSelectorConfig with tool_priority: [tool-a, tool-b], max_parallel: 4.

Scenario 1 (E601 backoff + fallback): Simulate tool-a failure with E601. Verify task gets delayed_until set. Verify ToolThrottleRegistry marks tool-a as throttled. Verify next dispatch selects tool-b. Verify effective_max_parallel decreased.
Scenario 2 (E602 hard block): Simulate tool-b failure with E602. Verify task transitions to Blocked state. Verify it is NOT retried.
Scenario 3 (recovery): Simulate successful completion on tool-a. Verify throttle cleared. Verify effective_max_parallel restored.
Ensure all assertions use the public API from rate_limit, task_selector, and engine modules.
Verify cargo test -p macc-core -- rate_limit_integration passes.
tasks.notes: This test does not require git or filesystem — it operates on in-memory TaskRegistry and ToolThrottleRegistry. Keep it fast.
